title: phelypaea ramosa subsp. nana
intro: Phelypaea ramosa subsp. nana, also known as dwarf phelypaea or dwarf poppy, is a small, annual plant in the poppy family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with its roots and leaves exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Phelypaea ramosa subsp. nana include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
